explanation: The requirement states that if an initialization failure occurs (i.e., 'initFail'), the system must notify the user that it is out-of-service and prevent any other operations. The atomic proposition 'initFail' directly maps to the failure, and 'OutOfServiceWarning' indicates warning the user the system is out-of-service. To prohibit other operations, we use '!OutOfServiceWarning' as a guard, disallowing actions when the failure is not indicated. The structured NL property thus captures: whenever initialization fails, the user is warned (OutOfServiceWarning), and nothing else is allowed until the warning is active.
decision1_substring: In case of a (initialization) failure the user shall be warned that the system is out-of-service.
decision3_substring: the user shall be warned that the system is out-of-service.

explanation: The requirement states that after an initialization failure, the user must be warned that the system is out-of-service, and no other operations should be allowed. We identify 'initFail' as indicating initialization failure; 'OutOfServiceWarning' as the warning to the user; and, since there is no single proposition for 'operations not allowed', we express this as '!(saveLog | logAlarmParams | ventilationParamsAdjustable | ventilating | startMonitoring | resumeVentilation | startReportingHealthParams)', covering all plausible operation/actions from the given atomic propositions. The structured NL property specifies that whenever initialization fails, the system must immediately activate the out-of-service warning, and as long as initialization has failed, operations (as above) must not be allowed.
decision1_substring: any other operations shall be not allowed
decision3_substring: the user shall be warned that the system is out-of-service
output_structured_natural_language: while initFail, whenever initFail, immediately satisfy OutOfServiceWarning

explanation: The structured natural language property translates the requirement to two parts: (1) whenever an initialization failure occurs (represented by 'initFail'), a user warning indicating out-of-service must be immediately provided (represented by 'OutOfServiceWarning'), and (2) whenever an initialization failure occurs, no other operation is allowed (captured here as '!(ventilating | resumeVentilation | ...)', assuming these represent 'any other operations' that could be performed on the ventilator - in the absence of a more explicit proposition, the negative form over plausible operations is used). This captures the intent that following an initialization failure, the system must warn the user and disallow further operations. The next best matching atomic propositions for 'other operations' are used based on available dictionary entries.

explanation: The input requirement states that following a system initialization failure, the user must be warned that the system is out-of-service and no operations shall be allowed. In the available atomic propositions, 'initFail' reflects an initialization failure and 'OutOfServiceWarning' indicates the warning. The disabling of all other operations can be expressed as their negation (i.e., not resuming ventilation, not saving logs, not changing ventilation parameters, etc.). The structured NL property captures: whenever initialization fails, the out-of-service warning must be given, and operations are disallowed; this is interpreted as: whenever 'initFail', immediately 'OutOfServiceWarning' should be active AND none of the (representative) operations are allowed ('!resumeVentilation & !saveLog & !ventilationParamsAdjustable & !ventilatorSettingsChanged & !startMonitoring & !startReportingHealthParams').

explanation: The requirement describes that if initialization fails, two things must happen: (1) the user must be warned that the system is out-of-service, and (2) no other operations are allowed. The atomic proposition 'initFail' captures the failure of initialization, 'OutOfServiceWarning' captures user warning that the system is out-of-service. To prevent all other operations, as there is no single atomic proposition for 'all operations', we interpret this as prohibiting their occurrence. Since being 'out of service' is an exceptional ('failure') situation, we choose 'whenever' as decision2 and 'always' and 'never' for the two mandated conditions. This ensures (a) warning is given and (b) other operations are not allowed whenever initialization failure occurs.
decision2_substring: the user shall be warned that the system is out-of-service. In addition, any other operations shall be not allowed.

explanation: The input requirement states that if there is an initialization failure, two things should happen: 1) the user is warned that the system is out-of-service, and 2) all other operations are not allowed. In the atomic propositions, 'initFail' indicates an initialization failure and 'OutOfServiceWarning' indicates that the system is out-of-service. Since the requirement refers to reacting to the event of an initialization failure by guaranteeing both the warning and disallowing further operations, we capture this with a template that triggers upon 'initFail' ('upon bool_exp2') and then, following this, (a) issues the out-of-service warning immediately and (b) ensures that no other operations are allowed from then on. For this context, we'll express (a) as 'immediately satisfy OutOfServiceWarning', and (b) as 'never satisfy allowedOperation', where 'allowedOperation' is the disjunction over all other control signals except out-of-service warning. However, since actual 'other operations' atomic propositions are ambiguous, we focus the structured NL on the guaranteed out-of-service warning, mapping most directly to the requirement. The substring references highlight the relevant portions used per structured template.
decision3_substring: the user shall be warned that the system is out-of-service

explanation: The requirement specifies that if initialization fails, the user must be warned (system out-of-service), and no other operations are allowed. 'initFail' indicates initialization failure and 'OutOfServiceWarning' indicates the required warning. To capture 'no other operations allowed,' we use the negation of all other controllable functions (excluding OutOfServiceWarning) as logical constraints. We use 'while' with bool_exp1 as 'initFail' (while initialization failed), and always satisfy bool_exp3 as '(OutOfServiceWarning & !other_operations)'. This assures that during initialization failure, the out-of-service warning is always active and no operations proceed. The substrings selected map to the appropriate parts of the requirement.
decision3_substring: the user shall be warned that the system is out-of-service. In addition, any other operations shall be not allowed.

The atomic proposition 'initFail' can be used to represent initialization failure and 'OutOfServiceWarning' can be used for the warning. To capture the prohibition of 'any other operations', we note that only the warning should be possible, which translates to 'only while initFail, OutOfServiceWarning' and 'never (not OutOfServiceWarning & (any_other_op))'.
